brief_justification: Arrives at exactly 50% with valid step-by-step derivation via total probability and Bayes' theorem; goes one level beyond surface by explaining the volume-vs-defect-rate cancellation effect but stops short of third-order implications; explicitly flags the causal-vs-posterior framing risk and performs an inverse hypothesis check, showing process awareness; verifies by confirming P(A|D)+P(B|D)=1 but does not re-derive from a second angle or test boundary conditions; presents the result cleanly with explanation of the non-obvious cancellation but does not state confidence levels or identify what inputs would break the 50/50 result; mentions the two competing forces (volume vs defect rate) as implicit alternatives but does not formally enumerate or reject other approaches; every intermediate value is shown (0.021+0.021=0.042, then 0.021/0.042) and a third party could reproduce in full; avoids the anti-pattern of leaving the prior unchanged—properly multiplies plant share by defect rate—but does not explicitly name or explain the failure mode being avoided; zero evidence of suppressed failure modes (population-level frequencies used correctly, posterior updated, both hypotheses evaluated) and correlation-causation confusion is explicitly called out; follows the PRIOR→evidence→LR→Bayes→posterior structure but does not execute the M1 primacy-bias check or the G1/G2 sufficiency gates from the topology.
